claim.content: Define $Z=m/m_{*}$ as the quasiparticle weight (with $m$ the noninteracting band mass and $m_{*}$ the quasiparticle effective mass) and $W$ as the noninteracting bare bandwidth ($W=4t$ for the Bethe lattice with hopping $t$). The effective low-energy scale (effective Fermi energy or Kondo temperature) is identified as $E_{F}^{*}\simeq Z W/2$, and for temperatures $T\leq T_{K}\equiv E_{F}^{*}$ the entropy per site of a Fermi liquid satisfies $S(T)/N\sim T/T_{K}$, so that evaluating at $T=T_{K}$ yields $S(T_{K})\sim O(1)$. This proposition asserts both the estimate $E_{F}^{*}\simeq ZW/2$ and the linear-in-$T$ low-temperature entropy scaling used to argue that the correlated metal near the MT carries an order-unity entropy at the scale $T_{K}$.
